content.full_text:
Fern Pourrcal PnacrrcEs CouurssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
April 19,2004
Linda Trask-Lee
1204 North B Street
Lompoc, CA93436
Re
Your Request for fnformal Assistance
Our File No. l-04-047
DemMs. Trask-Lee:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the campaign
reporting provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act').r Because your question is
general in nature and without regard to any identified expenditure we are providing
informal assistance.z 
"
QUESTION
What forms, if any, is your organization required to file with the Commission as a
result of its political activity?
CONCLUSION
If your orgarizationreceives contributions of $1,000 or more in a calendar year
for a political purposq it would qualifr as a "committee" and would be required to file a
Statement of Organization or Form 410. Once you have become a committeg you will
be required to report all contributions received, expenditures made, unpaid bills and any
miscellaneous increases to cash. This information wouldbe reported on a Recipient
Committee Campaign Statement, either the Form 450- Short Form, orthe Form 460 -
Long Form, depending on the type of information you have to report.
I Government Code sections 8 1000 - glll4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18-997, of the Califomia Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not proiide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Government Code $ 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. g 18329(c[3), copy enilosed.)


File No. I-04-047
Page No. 2
FACTS
You have formed a non-partisan organization for the primary purpose of
educating prospective candidates on how to organize campaigns and run for office. You
also inform voters on progressive issues and endorse progressive candidates.
You do not intend to give money to candidates or campaigrs, only your
endorsement. As your brochure indicates, your goal is as follows:
"Further equality and other values important to women through political,
social, and educational activities."
a
It is your intent to:
o Interview and endorse candidates who support your goals;
o Encourage and support appointrnents of qualified women to boards and
commissions;
o Engage in advocacy activities;
o Educate yourselves and your communities on issues of importance to
women;
o Increase the number of women in elected and appointed office at all levels
of government regardless of political affi liation.
In a telephone conversation with Commission staffon March 16,20A4, you stated
that you intend to accomplish these goals by sending out candidate questionnaires to
identiff candidate positions and inform the public of those positions by way of
newspaper ads or flyers. You may actually print the questionnaires and the candidates'
answers in such newspap€r ads or flyers and, if a candidate's answers to the questionnaire
indicate that he or she supports your organization's positions and goals, you may endorse
the candidate and publish that fact by way of newspaper advertisements or flyers. You
would mail the flyers to your membership and possibly distribute the flyers in other
public locations. You may publish a website, if someone in your organization possesses
the skills to design and maintain the site. You also may sponsor candidate or issue
forums on public access television, and you plan to sponsor candidate workshops on an
annual or serni-annual basis, that would teach the nuts and bolts ofhow to run for office.
These candidate workshops would be open to all members ofthe general public,
regardless of political views orpersuasion.
You expect your yearly income to be less than $2,000 and all of your income will
come from mernbership dues and member donations. In the Mmch 16,zo\4,telephone
conversation, you clarified that you expect the majority of your funds to be generated
from membership dues, with only a very small percentage from donations. Presently,
you are unincorporated; however, if you incorporate, you would prefer to incorporate as a
501(c)(4), but such a designation would not permit endorsing candidates or ballot
measures.


File No. I-04-047
Page No. 3
ANALYSIS
The campaign disclosure provisions of the Act require candidates and
"committees" to file periodic reports disclosing contributions received and expendifures
made. (Section 84100 et seq.) The term "committee" includes a person or organization
that receives contributions totaling $1,000 or more in a calendar year, (Section 82O13(a),
copy enclosed.)
A conkibution is defined in section 82015 (copy enclosed) as a payment made for
a political pu{pose. The regulations explain that payments made for a political purpose
include payments made:
'i[flor the purpose of influencing or attempting to influence the action of
the voters for or against the nomination oi election of a candidate or
candilgleg, or the qualification or passage of any measure." (Section
I 82 I 5(a)(1 ), copy enclosed.)
It appears that the major purpose of your organization would be to influence the
actions of the voters, albeit in a variety of ways. Your account ofthe facts indicates that
any dues or donation to your orgarization, made to further the organization's stated
pu{pose, would be payments for a political purpose, and therefore would be
"contributions" to your organization. Similarly, payme'nts made by your organization in
furtherance of its stated purpose would be "expenditures" as defined by the Act.
"Expenditure" is defined in section 82025, which states, in pertinent part:
"'Expenditure' means apayment, a forgiveness of a loan, a payment of a
loan by a third party, or an enforceable promise to make a pa5rment, unless
it is clear from the surrounding circumstances that it is not made for
political purposes."
Payments for political pu{poses also include "independent expenditures," which
are expenditures made in connection with a communication that expressly advocates the
election or defeat of a clearly identified candidate or the qualification, passage or defeat
of a clearly identified measure, that are not made directly to, or at the behest of, the
affected candidate or ballot measure committee.3 (Section 82031, copy enclosed.) Also
see enclosed 18225.7, defining "made at thebehest."
Applying these statutes and regulations to your organization, you must track
contributions received by your organization. Once contributions reach the sum of $1,000
ormore, you will be required to file a Statement of Organization orForm 410. Based on
the information you have provided, your organization would most likely be what is
known as a recipient committee.
3 Ho*"r"r, palm.ents for communications to members of your organization or their families for
the purpose ofsupporting or opposing a candidate or ballot measure are not contributions or expenditures,
"provided those palm.ents are not made for general public advertising such as broadcasting, billboards, and
newspaper advertisements. " (Section 8 53 12, copy enclosed.)


File No. I-04-047
Page No. 4
Once the organization qualifies as a coflrmittee under the Act, you will be
required to report all contributions received (including monetary and non-monetary
contributions, loans received, and enforceable promises); expenditures made (including
loans made); unpaid bills; and any miscellaneous increases to cash, such as interest.
Detailed itemization is required for: contributions received from a single source totaling,
in the aggregate, $100 or more in a calendar year; expenditures of $100 or more; accrued
expenses of $100 or more; and miscellaneous increases to cash of $100 or more. This
information will be reported on a Recipient Committee Campaign Statement, either Form
450 - Short Form, or Form 460 - Long Form, depending on the tlpe of information you
have to report.
Examples of these forms and further information about committee filing
requirements and deadlines can be found in Information Manual C, Campaign Provisions
of the Political Reform Act for: General Purpose Recipient Committees (Including
Sponsored Committees, Political Party Committees, and County Central Committees)
and the 2003 campaign Manual Addendum, copies ofwhich are enclosed.
If you have any other questions regarding this ma+iler,please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel
6
By: HollyB. Armstrong
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
Enclosures
HBAjg
